CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUN-11-2010 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 525, DEMURRER TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DEMURRER TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RESTITUTION IS SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DEMURRER TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION SEEKING A "WRIT OF MANDATE" IS ALSO SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO DISMISS. FURTHER ORDER OF THE COURT IS AS FOLLOWS; THE ACTION IS HEREBY DISMISSED WITH PREDJUDICE. (THE COURT'S ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO A SIMILAR ACTION BEING FILED BY A DIFFERENT PLAINTIFF (ONE OTHER THAN MR. MACK) WITH STANDING TO SUE AND GOOD CAUSE TO SUPPORT A CLAIM). JUDGE STEPHEN DOMBRINK (525)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2010 Continued To Apr-28-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA, COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is continued to April 28, 2010 to be heard at 9:30 am in Department 525. Judge Stephen Dombrink, retired from Alameda County, will hear the matter. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
